name: Yavalath
author: Cameron Browne / LUDI (2007) — AGP port
description: Cameron Browne's famous computer-designed (LUDI, 2007) misère/positive n-in-a-row on a hexagon of hexagons (side 5, 61 cells) — the hex analogue of Squava. Making four-in-a-row of your colour wins; making exactly three (not part of a four) loses; four takes precedence. A full board is a draw.
